backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor that composes blob storage and authorization mixins, manages user profiles, and defines Pokemon/battle types and game state structures for the ICP backend.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report confirming the drag-to-reposition dodge mechanic (replacing the dodge button) is implemented across AttackAnimation.tsx, PokemonBattleSprite.tsx, and Battle.tsx with high confidence.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document containing one-line summaries of all frontend source files, describing each page and component's purpose and notable implementation details.
frontend/index.css: Global Tailwind CSS entry point that defines CSS custom property design tokens for light and dark themes including colors, borders, and sidebar variables.
frontend/index.html: Minimal HTML shell for the React app titled 'Ash's Pokemon Journey' that mounts the app into a root div and loads the main TSX entry point.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component that configures TanStack Router with all application routes (login, intro, starter-selection, game, battle, story, gyms, roster, profile) under a shared Layout.
Reusable component that renders a Pokemon sprite (emoji or image) with configurable size, animation class, glow effect, optional horizontal flip, and sparkle particles.
Attack projectile animation component that checks the player Pokemon's position at impact time to determine if the attack was dodged (distance ≥ 45px from center), showing a miss effect or explosion accordingly and reporting the dodge result via onComplete.
frontend/src/components/BadgeDisplay.tsx: Component that fetches earned gym badges and renders a 4-column grid of all 8 gym badges, visually distinguishing earned vs. unearned badges with color and opacity.
frontend/src/components/EvolutionModal.t: A modal component that handles Pokémon evolution using an evolution stone, showing before/after stats and animating the evolution process via a mutation hook.
frontend/src/components/HealthBar.tsx: Displays a Pokémon's HP bar with color-coded fill (green/yellow/red) based on current health percentage, along with name and level.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Root layout component providing a sticky header with logo/logout, a bottom navigation bar for main game routes, and a footer; hides nav on battle/intro screens.
frontend/src/components/PokemonBattleSpr: Renders a Pokémon sprite in battle with support for drag-and-drop repositioning (mouse and touch) and CSS animation states like idle, attack, hit, faint, and dodge.
frontend/src/components/StrategyInput.ts: A text input component where players type battle strategies; it matches keywords to predefined responses and passes detected keywords to a parent callback.
frontend/src/components/TeamRocketEncoun: An encounter modal that plays through Team Rocket's motto line-by-line, then presents Battle or Flee options with a blast-off animation on fleeing.
frontend/src/data/pokemonData.ts: Defines TypeScript interfaces and local data for Pokemon and moves used in the battle system, including Pikachu as the strongest Pokemon (ATK=100) and a set of starter Pokemon with their stats and movesets.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Custom React hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor using React Query, supporting both anonymous and authenticated (Internet Identity) access, and invalidates dependent queries when the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an Internet Identity authentication context and provider component using DFINITY's AuthClient, exposing login/logout functions, identity state, and login status to child components.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Centralizes React Query hooks for all backend interactions including user profile, Pokemon, badges, story arc, and battle log CRUD operations using the ICP actor.
frontend/src/index.css: Global stylesheet that imports Google Fonts, configures Tailwind CSS with a Pokemon/anime theme including light/dark CSS variables, and defines custom utility classes and keyframe animations for battle effects.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that renders the React app wrapped with React Query's QueryClientProvider and the InternetIdentityProvider for authentication context.
frontend/src/pages/Battle.tsx: Full-featured Pokemon battle page component that manages turn-based combat state, player drag-to-dodge mechanics, attack animations, battle logging, and posts results to the backend via mutations.
frontend/src/pages/GameHome.tsx: Main game hub page that displays the trainer's home screen with quick battle, navigation options, and a random Team Rocket encounter. Handles profile setup modal for new users and redirects unauthenticated users to the login page.
frontend/src/pages/GymBattles.tsx: Gym battles listing page showing all gym leaders with their type, difficulty, and badge info. Allows the player to challenge a gym leader, triggering a confirmation modal before navigating to the battle screen.
frontend/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx: Login landing page that authenticates users via Internet Identity and redirects them to the intro or game home based on whether they have an existing profile. Displays animated stars, the app logo, and a Pikachu graphic.
frontend/src/pages/PokemonRoster.tsx: Pokémon roster page that displays the player's current Pokémon (loaded from sessionStorage) and allows evolution using compatible evolution stones from a simulated inventory. Opens an EvolutionModal on stone selection and updates the roster on confirmation.
frontend/src/pages/ProfessorOakIntro.tsx: Introductory cutscene page that plays through Professor Oak's classic dialogue with a typewriter effect. Advances through dialogue on click and navigates to the starter selection screen when complete.
frontend/src/pages/StarterSelection.tsx: Renders a starter Pokémon selection screen where the player picks one of several Pokémon, stores the choice in sessionStorage, and navigates to the game route after a confirmation animation.
frontend/src/pages/StoryCampaign.tsx: Displays a story campaign UI with selectable story arcs and episodes, tracking completion in sessionStorage and navigating to battles or showing a cinematic finale screen for the last episode.
frontend/src/pages/TrainerProfile.tsx: Shows the trainer's profile card with their name, principal ID, badge count, win/loss stats, and partner Pokémon, pulling data from backend queries and sessionStorage.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Provides utility functions for reading URL query parameters (supporting both standard and hash-based routing), persisting them to sessionStorage, and cleaning sensitive params from the URL hash.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration defining a Pokémon/anime-themed design system with custom colors (electric yellow, pikachu red, etc.), fonts (Bangers, Nunito), box shadows, border radii, and animation keyframes.
project_state.json: Tracks the overall project state for a Pokemon-style mobile RPG inspired by Ash Ketchum's journey, including architectural notes, known issues, and a list of existing implemented features with their IDs and versions.
